title: Cloud Run Revisions, Validation, Traffic, Rollback, and Recovery · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Cloud Run Rollback Rollforward
summary: A pipeline deploys latest, routes all traffic before readiness and smoke checks, changes IAM with application release, cannot identify the prior digest, and calls redeployment a rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cloud run rollback rollforward increment in progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes.
Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes. A defensible solution applies Ci Cloud Run Rollback Rollforward, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-cloud-run-rollback-rollforward — Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-3
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-3
    artifact: cloud-run-delivery.yaml
    decision: worked cd cloud run progressive delivery 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      deploy-cloudrun-action: 3
      service: status-api
      region: us-central1
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      revision-suffix: run8842
      traffic: none-at-deploy
      smoke: authenticated-changed-case
      canary-percent: 5
      observe: latency-errors-user-success
      abort: explicit-threshold
      rollback: prior-revision-traffic
      iam: separately-owned
    verify: inspect revision image identity readiness authenticated smoke traffic totals observation window abort decision rollback and IAM audit
    failure: Reject latest tags, deploy acceptance as readiness, instant full traffic, representative-canary assumptions, rebuild rollback, or deployment-owned public IAM.
    repair: Deploy verified digest without traffic, smoke exact revision, canary with signals, abort predictably, move traffic back, and separate IAM.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Redeploying old source is the fastest and most faithful rollback. This fails the Ci Cloud Run Rollback Rollforward contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
